audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in tavl.c are protected by either explicit NULL guards (root/allocation checks at L68, L69, L94, L200, L431) or the ldap_avl_child macro (which returns NULL for thread-bit links and is used in all traversal functions). The few direct avl_link accesses (L115 in insert, L339 in delete, L515/518-519 in next) rely on AVL tree invariants—avl_bits[dir]==AVL_CHILD implies avl_link[dir]!=NULL, and non-zero avl_bf implies a child exists—which are correctly maintained across all insert and delete paths.
